svg.title: Passage reverse TCP tunnel problem and solution diagram
svg.desc: A two-part diagram showing direct inbound access blocked by a private firewall, then Passage solving it with one outbound authenticated encrypted control connection from the private client to a public server, multiplexing user streams back to private services.

path.aria-label: passage: publish private TCP services through one secure outbound tunnel
A private workstation, appliance, or VLAN cannot safely accept direct inbound connections.
The private Passage client dials out once; the public server authenticates it and publishes only the allowed services.
yamux streams carry user TCP back through the tunnel
one outbound TLS 1.3 + X25519MLKEM768 control tunnel
Public users connect to SSH, DB, and HTTP ports; clients authenticate with required Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65 signatures.
